emotionalArcAchieved.pivotDescription: Lirien's hand on Kaelith's wrist during Ythara ontological exposure — physical contact as anchoring mechanism that collapses Kaelith back into singular selfhood

craftToolsApplied.application: Tone maintained as morally complex and atmospherically taut throughout. No melodrama during the Ythara exposure or vulnerability moment. The 'Thank you' scene is delivered with deliberate understatement.
Stakes escalate from theoretical god-threat to tangible ontological danger — Kaelith directly experiences what happens when reality unravels around a person.
Black Moment foreshadowing through deepening dependency — the trust being built here is what will make the ch16 betrayal revelation devastating.

Internal resistance: Kaelith cannot process dependence through tactical frameworks, must acknowledge need she cannot classify. External resistance: Conclave agents pursuing, Oathbrand limiting combat capacity.
Fantasy arc (Ythara exposure, Pallor damage) and romance arc (physical grounding, dependence acknowledgment) are fused — the fantasy threat creates the romantic vulnerability.

MOT-001 (Pallor as flayed skin — tears described as wounds, flesh swelling through bandage imagery). MOT-002 (Oathbrand as constant burn, screaming near tears, reacting to magic). MOT-004 (body-knowledge — combat coordination as instinctive architecture, Quietus path parallel).

Kaelith voice fingerprint maintained: tactical assessments, architectural/fortification metaphors, short declarative sentences, emotions as body events, inventory mode under stress, no water metaphors.

Emotional progression from determination → disorientation → acknowledged need → tactical partnership. No repeat of ch8's 'exposed fear' — this chapter's dominant emotion is conflicted desire and emerging dependence.

Vulnerability ladder advances: physical touch as anchoring (hand on wrist over pulse), verbal admission of need ('Thank you. For keeping me here.'), instinctive combat coordination revealing trust built below conscious awareness.

SCN-025.valueShift: theoretical god-threat → present and trying to unmake me
SCN-025.hookDelivered: reality-distortion — road forgetting itself, flagstones disagreeing on position

fiveCommandmentsHit.incitingIncident: Enter Wending where Pallor has catastrophically failed
fiveCommandmentsHit.complication: Kaelith experiences dissociation — hands not her hands, identity fracturing into multiple possibilities
fiveCommandmentsHit.crisis: Push through or retreat (implicit — no time to choose, the exposure hits)
fiveCommandmentsHit.climax: Lirien grips her wrist, speaks facts, anchors her to singular reality
fiveCommandmentsHit.resolution: Kaelith stabilizes; they discover mutual need for coherence in Ythara zones

SCN-026.valueShift: I survive alone → I need her to stay whole

fiveCommandmentsHit.incitingIncident: Kaelith attempts tactical processing of exposure and fails
fiveCommandmentsHit.complication: Lirien provides presence without demand — proximity without requiring engagement
fiveCommandmentsHit.crisis: Admit the dependence or maintain fiction of self-sufficiency
fiveCommandmentsHit.climax: Kaelith says 'Thank you. For keeping me here.' — closest she can come to admitting need
fiveCommandmentsHit.resolution: Lirien responds 'You would have done the same.' Both know it is true. Something fundamental shifts.

SCN-027.valueShift: we travel together → we fight as a unit
SCN-027.closeDelivered: aftermath-silence — walking into the Wending without speaking, silence holding what words cannot

fiveCommandmentsHit.complication: Kaelith weakened from Ythara exposure, fighting without magic against warded opponents
fiveCommandmentsHit.crisis: Surrender, fight alone, or fight coordinated
fiveCommandmentsHit.climax: Coordinated combat — Kaelith engages sword-bearer and caster, Lirien neutralizes third agent and enters at precise moment needed
fiveCommandmentsHit.resolution: Victory; agents down; they leave immediately knowing more will follow. Partnership has become military-grade coordination.

continuityNotes.characterPositions: Kaelith and Lirien departing waystation in the Wending, heading northwest on alternate route toward the Quietus after defeating three Conclave agents
continuityNotes.timeElapsed: One day of travel from Veldrane into the Wending
newInformation: Ythara's influence causes ontological fragmentation — identity splits into multiple possible selves, selfhood becomes probability rather than fact
Exposure duration to incapacitation: approximately 40 seconds without anchoring
Physical contact and factual grounding from a second person provides effective countermeasure
Pallor degradation in the Wending has exceeded Seep projections by 8-12 months
Conclave agents are magically armed (warded blades, caster with focus-rings)
Conclave documents suggest coordination with external parties beyond High Council authority
Kaelith and Lirien fight as instinctive coordinated unit without prior planning
foreshadowingPlanted: Ythara exposure sensory details (identity dissolution, multiple-self experience, screaming potential) — callbacks for climactic ch21 confrontation
Oathbrand resonating with Pallor tears — shared architecture between personal ward and divine membrane
Body-knowledge combat coordination as parallel to Quietus kinetic memory (MOT-004)
Conclave documents hinting at coordination beyond stated authority (FS-011 Seep agenda plant — Lirien withholds full information from documents)
foreshadowingAdvanced: FS-002: Pallor tears shown in severe state, escalating toward crisis
FS-009: Ythara's influence made tangibly terrifying through direct exposure
motifAppearances: MOT-001: Pallor tears described as wounds in flayed skin, flesh swelling through bandage cuts, membrane as dying skin
MOT-002: Oathbrand screaming near Pallor tears, burning chronically, resonating with shared architecture
MOT-004: Combat coordination as body-knowledge operating below conscious awareness, paralleled to Quietus path memory

informationAsymmetry.kaelithKnows: Ythara exposure is survivable with an anchor; Conclave agents tracking from Veldrane; Lirien withheld something from the captured documents
informationAsymmetry.kaelithDoesNotKnow: Full scope of Conclave's external coordination; Lirien's kill contract binding details; the full content of captured documents
informationAsymmetry.lirienKnows: Content of Conclave documents; her own Seep's divergent intelligence agenda; the kill contract she carries
informationAsymmetry.readerKnows: More than Kaelith about the kill contract scope; Lirien's personal investment beyond operations (from ch8 POV)

proseMetrics.averageSentenceLength: 8-12 words (shorter under stress, longer during reflection)
proseMetrics.dialogueToNarrationRatio: 15:85 — heavily narration-focused chapter reflecting Kaelith's interiority and the experiential Ythara exposure
